senses.definition: The set of formal or specialized terms and expressions used in professional contexts, or the state of being a professional in attitude or conduct.
examples: The journal includes a section on the linguistics of professionalisms in medical writing.
Her speech was full of professionalisms that only doctors would understand.

senses.definition: A person who acts or behaves as a professional; a person who relentlessly adheres to professional standards or practices.
examples: He is seen as a professionalist, always following the rules and best practices.
Some critics call him a professionalist rather than an innovator.

senses.definition: Multiple people who act as professionals or who adhere strictly to professional standards.
examples: The conference drew several professionalists from the tech industry.
Professionalists often push for higher standards in their field.

senses.definition: The quality of being professional in appearance, behavior, and conduct.
examples: Her professionality impressed the clients.
The team values discipline and professionality.

senses.definition: The process of making a job or activity into a true profession with formal training, standards, and recognition.
examples: The professionalization of nursing improved patient care.
There is ongoing debate about the professionalization of teaching.

senses.definition: Multiple instances or processes of professionalization across fields or organizations.
examples: Different sectors undergo various professionalizations and reforms.
Policy changes aimed at professionalizations gained momentum.

senses.definition: To make something more professional in standards, training, or appearance; to introduce formal procedures or qualifications.
examples: The hospital will professionalize its training programs.
They aim to professionalize the service by introducing certifications.

senses.definition: Having been made more professional in standards or behavior; already subject to formal qualifications or procedures.
examples: The workforce is highly professionalized.
This program is fully professionalized with accredited trainers.

senses.definition: Third-person singular form of professionalize; makes something more professional.
examples: The new policy professionalizes the industry standard.
It professionalizes how we train apprentices.

senses.definition: The act of making something more professional through standards, training, or behavior.
examples: They are professionalizing the recruitment process.
Professionalizing the field requires new accreditation.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to a profession or shows professional behavior; with skill and competence.
examples: She spoke professionally about the project.
The team behaves professionally under pressure.

senses.definition: People who are trained and qualified in a specific profession; experts who provide skilled services.
examples: The conference attracted many healthcare professionals.
She joined a group of professionals to work on the project.

senses.definition: A person who adheres strictly to professional standards; a professional by practice, sometimes with a critical nuance.
examples: Critics described him as a professionist who focused on rules over innovation.

senses.definition: To make something into a recognized profession with standards and qualifications.
examples: There is a push to professionize the teaching field.

senses.definition: Having no professional status or standards; operating without recognized professional credentials.
examples: A professionless market could lead to inconsistent quality.
They warned about professionless practices in the industry.

senses.definition: Jobs or fields of work that require specialized training and professional standards.
examples: Nursing and teaching are both lifelong professions.
The professions often have codes of ethics.

senses.definition: Relating to profess or professing; not commonly used in modern English; may appear in older or niche contexts.
examples: The text uses professive language typical of medieval treatises.

senses.definition: In a professive manner; rarely used in modern English.
examples: The author writes professively, with formal cadence.

senses.definition: A senior academic teacher at a college or university, often a researcher.
examples: She met with a professor to discuss her thesis.
Professors publish in scholarly journals.

senses.definition: The body or rank of professors at a university; the position of professor collectively.
examples: The professorate at the university includes several renowned researchers.

senses.definition: Multiple professorates; the collective rank or bodies of professors at several institutions.
examples: Different universities have varying professorates.

senses.definition: The realm, dignity, or community of professors; a traditional or humorous way to refer to the professoriate.
examples: Her work within the professorate earned her wide respect.

senses.definition: A female professor; an older or less common term for a woman who is a professor.
examples: The professoress gave a compelling lecture.

senses.definition: Plural form of professoress; multiple female professors.
examples: The conference featured several professoresses from across the country.

senses.definition: The status or position of being a professor; the collective body of professors.
examples: He joined the professorhood with a distinguished record.
